feed.title: Learning with Hanyu
feed.subtitle: All models are wrong, but some are useful

entry.title: R
entry.content:
Introduction

Termology

Script; Command; Comment; Variable; Fun; Argument
How to put arguments into a fun?
• Check the help page
• Order for arguments (not required, but recommend)
• Know default for arguments
Do I have to set/enter all arguments?

Synatax
• A set of rules

Packages
• A collection of R funs
• Base: function name{base}
• Base cheat sheet
○ www.rstudio.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/10/r-cheat-sheet-3.pdf
• Install packages
○ Install.package()
○ Library()
○ [packagename]::[fun] eg. devtools::check()
• Where can I find R packages?
○ CRAN
○ Bioconduct ???
○ Github
• Most used packages
○ https://towardsdatascience.com/top-100-most-used-r-functions-on-github-9caf2b81b314
○

Working directory
• getwd()
• setwd()

R project
• A collection of files (data; results; graphs)
• To keep your analysis organized
○ Data; papers; rcode; writing; Rproj; Readme
• Relative path: use to do reproductive work and share
• What files do you have in this folder?
○ .Rhistory
○ .Rproj
○ Auto-saved

Help in R
• ?funname
• ?”special characters”
• help(package = “package name”)
• example(function name)

Data Type
• Most common atomic data type
○ Numeric: 1, 2.3, 14.78
○ Integers
○ Complex
○ Logical
○ Characters
• Find out the data type
○ class(2)
○ class(4L)
○ class(1+2i)
○ class(TRUE)
• Change data type later too
○ as.numeric()
○ Remember some cant be changed or in an error way!!!

Data structure
• Data Frames
○ Create a data frame
§ data.frame()
§ Load mtcars data frame
□ data() # to see the different datasets within R
□ ?mtcars
§ Useful commands
□ str() # find out the stucture of a data frame
□ head()
□ head(mtcars,10) # list the first 6 rows of data (or you could specific how many rows you want to preview)
□ tail() # list the last 6 rows of data (or you could specific how many rows you want to preview)
§ Subset
□ [row, column]
□ [“var name”]
□ df$var
® mtcars[3,2]
® mtcars[3, ]
® mtcars[3:7,2]
□ Use subset() to select data (ex: mpg >15)
® subset(mtcars, mpg > 15)

Explore data

How do I select data?
• subset()
• comparision (>;<;>=;<=;==;!)
• R logical operators
○ !x
○ x&y
○ x | y

entry.title: Learning with Hanyu
entry.content:
Welcome to Learning with Hanyu. This is my very first post.

Today, I want to share a pome with you (•͈˽•͈)

如果给你寄一本书
我不会寄给你诗歌
我要给你一本关于植物，关于庄稼的
告诉你稻子和稗子的区别
告诉你一棵稗子提心吊胆的
春天
———余秀华《我爱你》
entry.summary:
Welcome to Learning with Hanyu. This is my very first post.

Today, I want to share a pome with you (•͈˽•͈)
